title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Runtime Contract: Oai Oac Migration
summary: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oai oac migration increment in private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
Grant the exact distribution use of the KMS key separately from S3 bucket access and verify encrypted read and write cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Oai Oac Migration · Theory Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1301 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
